title: ES Mock 09 — Full Syllabus High Difficulty

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the option that correctly completes the sentence: 'By the time the rescue team arrived, the trapped miners ____ for nearly ten hours.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An action continuing up to a point in the past uses the past perfect continuous tense.

**Working.** The reference point ('the team arrived') is in the past, and the waiting was ongoing before it. This requires 'had been + verb-ing'.

**Answer.** had been waiting.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the correct article: 'The supervisor is ____ honest engineer who never compromises on safety.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The choice between 'a' and 'an' depends on the sound, not the spelling, of the following word.

**Working.** 'Honest' begins with a silent 'h', so it starts with a vowel sound, requiring 'an'.

**Answer.** an.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The simple interest on $5000$ at $8\%$ per annum for $3$ years is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{SI}=\dfrac{P\,r\,t}{100}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{5000\times 8\times 3}{100}=1200$$

**Answer.** $1200$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A sector representing $25\%$ of a total is shown shaded on the pie chart. Compute its central angle (degrees).

figure.caption: Pie sector
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M85 80 L85 20 A60 60 0 0 1 145 80 Z' fill='#2563eb'/><text x='30' y='158' font-size='8'>shaded = 25% of pie</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Central angle $=\text{fraction}\times360^\circ$.

**Working.**
$$\theta=0.25\times360=90^\circ$$

**Answer.** $90^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Spatial Aptitude — Paper Folding and Punching
questions.stem: A square sheet is folded once in half and a single hole is punched through both layers. When unfolded, the number of holes is _____ .

figure.caption: A square folded once in half with one hole punched through the folded sheet.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='20' y='20' width='60' height='70' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.10)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='80' y1='20' x2='80' y2='90' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><circle cx='50' cy='55' r='6' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each fold layer carries one hole.

**Working.** One fold gives $2$ layers; one punch makes $1\times 2 = 2$ holes.

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The arrow shown points to the right (east). After rotating it $90^\circ$ anticlockwise, it points

figure.caption: A horizontal arrow pointing to the right.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='40' x2='120' y2='40' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='120,40 105,32 105,48' fill='#ef4444'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $90^\circ$ anticlockwise turn sends east to north.

**Working.** Right rotated anticlockwise becomes upward.

**Answer.** Upward (north).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The arrow shown points upward (north). After rotating it $180^\circ$, it points

figure.caption: A vertical arrow pointing upward.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='40' y1='130' x2='40' y2='30' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='40,30 32,45 48,45' fill='#10b981'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $180^\circ$ rotation reverses the direction.

**Working.** Upward becomes downward.

**Answer.** Downward (south).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A bag contains $3$ red and $2$ blue balls. The probability of drawing a red ball at random is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Probability = favourable / total.

**Working.** $\dfrac{3}{3+2}=\dfrac{3}{5}$.

**Answer.** $\dfrac{3}{5}$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: When the letter shown is viewed in a vertical mirror placed to its right, the mirror image looks like the letter

figure.caption: The lowercase letter b with a dashed vertical mirror line on its right.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='45' y='88' font-size='80' fill='#334155'>b</text><line x1='130' y1='12' x2='130' y2='108' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A vertical mirror swaps left and right.

**Working.** The bowl of 'b' is on the right; reflecting it to the left turns the shape into 'd'.

**Answer.** d.

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: Which conditions worsen ground-level air pollution by limiting dispersion?

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="10" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">altitude</text><text x="245" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">temperature →</text><line x1="120" y1="160" x2="220" y2="30" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3" stroke-dasharray="5 3"/><text x="222" y="34" font-size="8" fill="#475569">DALR (dry adiabatic)</text><line x1="150" y1="160" x2="200" y2="30" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.6"/><text x="172" y="50" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">ELR (inversion)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Environmental lapse rate vs. dry adiabatic lapse rate — inversion atmosphere.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Inversions, low mixing height and calm winds all trap pollutants; a strong superadiabatic lapse improves dispersion.

**Working.**
First three.

**Answer.** Inversion, low mixing height, calm winds

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: Which processes are strictly anaerobic in wastewater treatment?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Methanogenesis requires strict anaerobiosis. Denitrification is anoxic (uses NO₃⁻), nitrification and carbon oxidation are aerobic.

**Working.**
Only methanogenesis is strictly anaerobic.

**Answer.** Methanogenesis

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: The optimum carbon-to-nitrogen (C/N) ratio for efficient aerobic composting is approximately
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A C/N around 25–30:1 balances microbial energy (C) and protein synthesis (N).

**Working.**
≈ 25–30:1.

**Answer.** 25–30:1

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked. Assuming 10% transfer per trophic level, the energy available at trophic level $4$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 180" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><polygon points="40,150 280,150 240,118 80,118" fill="#16a34a" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="80,118 240,118 205,86 115,86" fill="#65a30d" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="115,86 205,86 178,54 142,54" fill="#d97706" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="142,54 178,54 165,30 155,30" fill="#dc2626" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="160" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">Producers = 10000</text><text x="160" y="110" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L2 = ?</text><text x="160" y="78" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L3 = ?</text><text x="160" y="46" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L4 = ?</text><text x="160" y="172" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kcal/m²/yr — 10% transfer per level</text></svg>
figure.caption: Only the producer (trophic level 1) energy is marked; upper levels follow the 10% rule. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each transfer retains ~10%: $E_n=E_0(0.1)^n$, with $E_0$ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$10000\times0.1^{3}=10.$$

**Answer.** $10\,\text{kcal/m}^2/\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Decomposable waste of $2000\,\text{kg}$ yields landfill gas at $0.1\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg}$. The total gas generated is _____ m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="86" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.5"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><rect x="30" y="126" width="280" height="14" fill="#1e3a8a" opacity="0.6"/><rect x="30" y="140" width="280" height="10" fill="#0f172a"/><text x="170" y="26" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">final cover / vegetation</text><text x="170" y="86" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">compacted MSW + daily cover</text><text x="170" y="137" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">leachate collection</text><text x="170" y="148" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">composite liner (clay+HDPE)</text><path d="M315 60 q14 0 14 14 q0 10 -8 18" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="318" y="56" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">gas vent</text></svg>
figure.caption: Engineered sanitary landfill: cover, waste cell, liner and leachate system.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gas $=$ mass $\times$ specific yield.

**Working.**
$$2000\times0.1=200.$$

**Answer.** $200\,\text{m}^3$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 600 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 2.5 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{2.5\,\text{m}}{600\,\text{s}}\times1000=4.17\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $4.17\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: Which device is most effective for removing very fine particulates (< 1 µm) at high efficiency?

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 280 200" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><path d="M70 40 L210 40 L210 110 L165 175 L115 175 L70 110 Z" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.06)" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="120" y="20" width="40" height="22" fill="#fff" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="140" y="16" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">clean gas out</text><path d="M40 55 l30 0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="50" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">dusty in</text><path d="M150 50 q30 25 -8 50 q-34 18 6 45" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="130" y="190" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#7c2d12">dust hopper</text></svg>
figure.caption: Reverse-flow cyclone separator (vortex collects particulates).

questions.solution:
**Concept.** ESPs and fabric filters capture sub-micron particles; settling chambers/cyclones are for coarse particles.

**Working.**
ESP.

**Answer.** Electrostatic precipitator

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $3$ identical sources each $75\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$75+10\log3=79.77.$$

**Answer.** $79.77\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: Acid rain is chiefly caused by the atmospheric oxidation and hydration of
options: SO₂ and NOₓ to sulphuric and nitric acids
questions.solution:
**Concept.** SO₂→H₂SO₄ and NOₓ→HNO₃ depress rain pH below ~5.6.

**Working.**
SO₂ and NOₓ.

**Answer.** SO₂ and NOₓ

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="40" width="250" height="90" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M20 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="14" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">Q = 8 MLD</text><path d="M290 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="300" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">out</text><line x1="40" y1="48" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="120" cy="80" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="170" cy="100" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="210" cy="118" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><path d="M120 86 L132 124 M170 106 L182 126" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="0.8"/><text x="120" y="150" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">plan area A = 400 m²</text><text x="160" y="36" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">surface (overflow)</text></svg>

**Concept.** SOR = Q/A, with Q and A read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{8\times1000}{400}=20.$$

**Answer.** $20$

questions.stem: The stable, self-perpetuating community reached at the end of ecological succession is the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Succession culminates in a climax community in equilibrium with the environment.

Climax community.

**Answer.** Climax community

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: For the landfill shown, read the usable air-space and the annual fill rate from the diagram. Its design life is _____ years.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="100" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.45"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><text x="170" y="86" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">air-space = 5,00,000 m³</text><text x="170" y="108" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">fill rate = 40,000 m³/yr</text><text x="170" y="166" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">design life = air-space ÷ fill rate</text></svg>

**Concept.** Life = air-space/annual fill, both read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{500000}{40000}=12.5.$$

**Answer.** $12.5\,\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: First-order decay, $k=0.231\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** t½ = ln2/k.

**Working.**
$$0.693/0.231=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{d}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $3\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 265</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$3\times265=795.$$

**Answer.** $795$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A direct consequence of eutrophication in a water body is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Algal blooms die and decompose, consuming DO and raising BOD.

**Working.**
DO depletion.

**Answer.** Oxygen depletion

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Under colour-coded biomedical-waste segregation, anatomical/infected waste for incineration is typically placed in the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Yellow = incinerable anatomical/soiled waste; red = contaminated recyclable plastics; blue/white = glass/sharps.

**Working.**
Yellow bag.

**Answer.** Yellow

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ achieved is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="114" width="50" height="36" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="110" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">120</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">400</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{120}{400}\right)\times100=70\%.$$

**Answer.** $70\%$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A hyperaccumulator crop yields $20\,\text{t/ha}$ of dry biomass containing $500\,\text{mg/kg}$ of nickel. The nickel phytoextracted per hectare in one harvest is _____ g/ha.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Metal extracted $=$ dry biomass $\times$ tissue concentration. With biomass $20\,\text{t/ha}=20{,}000\,\text{kg/ha}$ and $500\,\text{mg/kg}$: $(20{,}000)(500)/1000$ g.

**Working.**
$$20{,}000\times500/1000=10{,}000\,\text{g/ha}.$$

**Answer.** $10\,000\,\text{g/ha}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: The cyclical framework underpinning an ISO 14001 EMS is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** ISO 14001 follows the PDCA continual-improvement loop.

**Working.**
PDCA.

**Answer.** Plan-Do-Check-Act

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{2.5\,\text{m}}{400\,\text{s}}\times1000=6.25\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $6.25\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: In a reactor, $40\,\text{mg/L}$ of biomass is produced while $100\,\text{mg/L}$ of substrate is consumed. The observed yield $Y_{X/S}$ is _____ mg/mg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Y_{X/S}=\dfrac{\Delta X}{\Delta S}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{40}{100}=0.4.$$

**Answer.** $0.4$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays by first-order kinetics with rate constant $k=0.1\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Its half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For first order, $t_{1/2}=\dfrac{\ln 2}{k}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.1}=6.93.$$

**Answer.** $6.93\,\text{d}$

**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Answer.** $0.4$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Using the modified Dulong formula $HHV = 337C + 1419(H - O/8) + 93S$ (kJ/kg), a fuel with $C=45\%$, $H=6\%$, $O=40\%$, $S=0\%$ has a higher heating value of _____ kJ/kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula with percentages.

**Working.**
$$337(45)+1419(6-40/8)+93(0)=16584\,\text{kJ/kg}.$$

**Answer.** $16584\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1000\,\text{kg}$ at $60\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1000(1-60/100)=400.$$

**Answer.** $400\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $6\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹1,20,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 4</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120000}{(1+0.06)^{4}}=95051.$$

**Answer.** $₹95051$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Which characteristics define a hazardous waste under typical regulatory frameworks?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The four hazard characteristics are ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity and toxicity; moisture content is not one.

**Working.**
Ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity.

**Answer.** Ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A water contains $40\,\text{mg/L}$ of $\text{Ca}^{2+}$ and $12\,\text{mg/L}$ of $\text{Mg}^{2+}$. The total hardness as $\text{CaCO}_3$ is _____ mg/L. (Eq. wt: Ca 20, Mg 12.15, CaCO₃ 50)

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="18" x2="38" y2="160" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="160" x2="305" y2="160" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="14" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="240" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant added →</text><line x1="38" y1="89" x2="305" y2="89" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 3"/><text x="42" y="86" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">pH 7</text><path d="M38 150 C120 146 160 140 168 89 C176 38 230 32 305 28" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="168" cy="89" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence pt</text></svg>
figure.caption: EDTA titration is used to measure total hardness.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to $\text{CaCO}_3$: $\text{mg/L}\times\dfrac{50}{\text{eq.wt}}$.

**Working.**
$$40\times\dfrac{50}{20}+12\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=149.4.$$

**Answer.** $149.4\,\text{mg/L as CaCO}_3$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows logistically at $r=0.4\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the carrying capacity $K$ from the growth curve shown, the maximum $dN/dt$ ($rK/4$) is _____ /yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="34" y1="20" x2="34" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="40" x2="305" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="240" y="36" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">K = 8000</text><path d="M34 158 C120 156 150 80 175 60 C210 44 260 41 305 40" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><text x="10" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="285" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><text x="120" y="120" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">max dN/dt at N=K/2</text></svg>
figure.caption: Carrying capacity K marked on the growth curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** max dN/dt = rK/4 at N=K/2, with K read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.4\times8000}{4}=800.$$

**Answer.** $800$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A river has DO saturation $8\,\text{mg/L}$. From the oxygen-sag curve shown, read the critical deficit $D_c$. The minimum dissolved oxygen in the stream is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 200" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><line x1="34" y1="30" x2="34" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="175" x2="330" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="8" y="40" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">DO</text><text x="305" y="190" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">distance/time →</text><line x1="34" y1="45" x2="330" y2="45" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="250" y="42" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">saturation DO</text><path d="M34 60 Q120 175 175 150 Q260 110 330 70" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="175" y1="45" x2="175" y2="150" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="175" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="180" y="120" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">D_c = 4 mg/L</text><text x="150" y="170" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">t_c = 2.5 d</text></svg>
figure.caption: Critical deficit D_c (and t_c) marked on the sag curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Minimum DO = DO_sat − D_c, with D_c read from the sag curve.

**Working.**
$$8-4=4.$$

**Answer.** $4\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Modified Dulong $HHV=337C+1419(H-O/8)$ kJ/kg with $C=40\%$, $H=7\%$, $O=40\%$. HHV is _____ kJ/kg.

figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(40)+1419(7-40/8)=16318.$$

**Answer.** $16318\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: For the settling tank shown, read the flow $Q$ (MLD) and plan area $A$ from the diagram. The surface overflow rate $Q/A$ is _____ m³/m²/day.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** SOR = Q/A, with Q and A read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{10\times1000}{400}=25.$$

**Answer.** $25$

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 350 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="60" y="40" width="110" height="70" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="115" y="78" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Aeration</text><text x="115" y="92" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">MLSS, V</text><circle cx="240" cy="75" r="34" fill="rgba(22,163,74,0.10)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="240" y="72" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Clarifier</text><path d="M20 70 l40 0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="22" y="62" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">Q, S0</text><path d="M170 70 l36 0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M274 70 l44 0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="290" y="62" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">Qe, Se</text><path d="M240 109 l0 36 -180 0 0 -35" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="120" y="158" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">return sludge Qr</text><path d="M250 140 l40 0" stroke="#7c2d12" stroke-width="1.2"/><text x="255" y="135" font-size="8" fill="#7c2d12">waste Qw</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** F/M = QS₀/(VX).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{12000\times250}{2000\times3000}=0.5.$$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays first-order with $k=0.3\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Reading the initial concentration $C_0$ from the figure, its concentration after $8$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 170" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="120" y="100" width="60" height="50" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.25" stroke="#1e293b"/><rect x="120" y="30" width="60" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t = 0</text><text x="150" y="96" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">C₀ = 100</text><text x="150" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial concentration C₀ marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$100e^{-0.3\times8}=9.07.$$

**Answer.** $9.07$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows logistically at $r=0.2\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the carrying capacity $K$ from the growth curve shown, the maximum $dN/dt$ ($rK/4$) is _____ /yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="34" y1="20" x2="34" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="40" x2="305" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="240" y="36" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">K = 8000</text><path d="M34 158 C120 156 150 80 175 60 C210 44 260 41 305 40" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><text x="10" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="285" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><text x="120" y="120" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">max dN/dt at N=K/2</text></svg>
figure.caption: Carrying capacity K marked on the growth curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** max dN/dt = rK/4 at N=K/2, with K read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.2\times8000}{4}=400.$$

**Answer.** $400$
